lesson: Use cache-aside with a TTL, not write-through alone: on write, invalidate the key rather than update it, bounding staleness if the invalidation message itself is lost
Attach a client-generated idempotency key to every mutating request and dedupe server-side, so a client retry after a timeout can't double-charge or double-insert
Shard with consistent hashing instead of key mod N: adding or removing a shard remaps only ~1/N of keys instead of nearly all of them
After a user's write, pin their next reads to the primary (read-your-writes) instead of the replica pool, or async replication lag will show them stale data
Load-balance with least-outstanding-requests or power-of-two-choices instead of round robin when request costs vary, since round robin sends equal load to slow and fast backends
Decide AP vs CP per operation, not per system: a checkout's inventory decrement stays CP during a partition while its product-view page stays AP for availability
Design queue consumers for at-least-once delivery plus idempotent processing rather than chasing broker-guaranteed exactly-once, which no distributed queue truly delivers end-to-end
Prevent cache stampede on a hot key's expiry with request coalescing (single-flight) or probabilistic early recomputation, so one expiry doesn't trigger thousands of simultaneous origin fetches
Size shard count from peak QPS times a 2-3x headroom multiplier divided by per-node throughput, not from average traffic, so the design survives traffic spikes without a fire-drill resplit
